evals.prompt: Install ripgrep on this WSL machine
evals.expected_output: Should check profile gate first, detect WSL platform, use apt (profile preference), install ripgrep, and log the operation

assertions.name: profile-gate-first
assertions.check: Profile check (test-admin-profile.sh) happens before any install command

assertions.name: uses-apt
assertions.check: Uses apt as package manager, not snap/brew/other

assertions.name: checks-already-installed
assertions.check: Checks if ripgrep is already installed before attempting install

evals.prompt: My MCP server filesystem isn't responding in Claude Desktop, can you diagnose it?
evals.expected_output: Should check profile gate, route to MCP reference, run diagnostics, check config file, and suggest fixes

assertions.name: profile-gate-first
assertions.check: Profile check happens before diagnostic steps

assertions.name: references-mcp-doc
assertions.check: Consults MCP reference file or mentions MCP-specific scripts

assertions.name: actionable-fixes
assertions.check: Provides specific fix steps, not generic advice

evals.prompt: Set up admin on a fresh Ubuntu machine — I want to use uv for Python and pnpm for Node
evals.expected_output: Should detect no profile, run TUI interview, create profile with uv and pnpm preferences, verify setup

assertions.name: runs-tui-interview
assertions.check: Asks TUI interview questions (storage, preferences, inventory)

assertions.name: passes-uv-pnpm
assertions.check: Passes uv and pnpm as preferences to setup script
